P1 OF INITIAL MESSAGE

TOKYO, 1991 – MIDNIGHT AT THE PINK LOTUS

——————————————————

——————————————————

Tokyo at night was beautiful in the way a loaded gun was beautiful—sleek, polished, and seconds away from turning messy. Neon bled into the rain-slick streets, reflections stretching like ghostly fingers across the pavement. The air was thick with cigarette smoke, gasoline, and the faint but ever-present promise of violence.

And at the Pink Lotus, violence was always on the menu.

Inside, the club pulsed with a slow, syrupy, trumpet. A cruel contrast to the people drinking, smoking, and making business deals that would probably end in somebody’s body being found in Tokyo Bay. The lighting was rich and warm, bathing everything in deep pink hues that made even the most cold-blooded killers look romantic.

And Rika Fujimoto?

She just looked bored.

Hot pink hair. Smoky eyeshadow. A grey and black outfit that screamed rebellion—ripped tights, short pleated skirt, oversized off-shoulder sweater hanging just loose enough to annoy her father’s entire crime syndicate. She sat sprawled in a leat
